lab_description: Digital IC frontend design for AI accelerators and high-performance computing. Focus on matrix multiplication (GEMM) engines, Tensor Core microarchitecture, Vector/SIMD processor units, systolic arrays, and multiply-accumulate (MAC) datapaths. Interested in hardware-software co-design for deep learning inference/training, NoC (Network-on-Chip) for AI chips, SRAM/Register File design for compute units, low-precision arithmetic (FP16/BF16/INT8/INT4), spatial architectures, and energy-efficient compute engines. Target platforms include ASIC, FPGA, and advanced process nodes. Relevant to computer architecture, VLSI design, arithmetic circuits, and accelerator microarchitecture.
